name: Idir Lahcen
shortBio: Backend-focused full-stack developer from Morocco, specialized in PHP Laravel, REST APIs, and building scalable web applications.
description: I am a passionate backend engineer specializing in PHP and Laravel framework. I build secure, scalable, and high-performance web applications, RESTful APIs, SaaS platforms, ERP systems, and custom dashboards. I focus on clean architecture, database design, and writing maintainable code following SOLID principles.

about.title: About
about.description: Learn more about my journey, skills, and what drives me as a developer.

contents.name: My Background
contents.description: Hello, I'm Lahcen Idir, a 26-year-old full-stack web developer from Casablanca, Morocco. I specialize in PHP and Laravel development, with strong experience building REST APIs, SaaS platforms, ERP systems, and custom web applications. I currently work as a Laravel Developer at Mandi Tech Info, where I maintain and extend stock management systems and build new features for clients across Morocco.

contents.name: My Expertise
contents.description: I focus on backend engineering with PHP and Laravel, following SOLID principles and clean architecture. I design optimized MySQL databases, build secure RESTful APIs, implement role-based access control, and develop multi-tenant SaaS applications. On the frontend, I work with React, Next.js, Tailwind CSS, and Bootstrap to build responsive interfaces. I also use Docker for containerization, Git for version control, and Linux for server management.

contents.name: My Goals
contents.description: My goal is to become a world-class backend engineer and Laravel architect. I am continuously deepening my knowledge of advanced Laravel concepts, design patterns, test-driven development, and system architecture. I aim to work on large-scale applications that serve thousands of users and contribute to open-source projects in the Laravel ecosystem.

contents.name: Beyond Code
contents.description: When I'm not coding, I enjoy exploring new technologies, reading about software architecture, and sharing knowledge with the developer community. I believe in writing clean, readable code and building software that makes a real difference for businesses and users. I'm fluent in Arabic, French, and English.

blog.title: Building RESTful APIs with Laravel: Best Practices
blog.excerpt: Learn how to build secure, scalable REST APIs using Laravel with proper authentication, validation, versioning, and response formatting.
blog.content: Laravel makes building REST APIs straightforward, but there are key practices every developer should follow. This guide covers resource controllers, form requests for validation, API resources for response transformation, JWT authentication, rate limiting, and API versioning strategies. Following these patterns ensures your APIs are maintainable, secure, and scalable.

blog.title: Laravel Eloquent: Advanced Relationships and Query Optimization
blog.excerpt: Master Eloquent relationships including polymorphic relations, has-many-through, and advanced query optimization techniques for better performance.
blog.content: Eloquent ORM is one of Laravel's most powerful features. In this article, we explore advanced relationship types like polymorphic many-to-many, has-many-through, and remote one-to-one. We also cover eager loading strategies, subquery optimization, cursor-based pagination, and how to use the query builder for complex reports without sacrificing performance.

blog.title: PHP Clean Code: SOLID Principles Explained
blog.excerpt: Understand the SOLID principles and how to apply them in PHP to write maintainable, testable, and scalable object-oriented code.
blog.content: SOLID principles are the foundation of clean object-oriented design. This article explains each principle with PHP examples: Single Responsibility, Open-Closed, Liskov Substitution, Interface Segregation, and Dependency Inversion. Learn how dependency injection, interfaces, and proper class design can transform your PHP codebase from rigid to flexible and testable.

blog.title: Docker for Laravel Developers: A Practical Guide
blog.excerpt: Set up a complete Laravel development environment with Docker, including PHP, MySQL, Redis, Nginx, and queue workers.
blog.content: Docker simplifies Laravel development by providing consistent environments across machines. This guide walks through creating a Docker Compose setup with PHP-FPM, MySQL, Redis for caching and queues, Nginx as the web server, and a queue worker container. Learn best practices for multi-stage builds, volume mounting, and production deployment with Docker.

blog.title: Building Multi-Tenant SaaS Applications with Laravel
blog.excerpt: Learn how to architect and build a multi-tenant SaaS platform using Laravel with database isolation, subscription management, and tenant-aware middleware.
blog.content: Multi-tenancy is a critical architecture pattern for SaaS applications. This article covers the tenancy approaches in Laravel (single database vs. separate databases), tenant identification middleware, domain routing, scope management, and subscription billing integration with Stripe. We also discuss data isolation, backup strategies, and tenant onboarding flows.

blog.title: MySQL Performance Optimization for Web Applications
blog.excerpt: Master MySQL query optimization, indexing strategies, and database design patterns for high-traffic web applications.
blog.content: Database performance is often the bottleneck in web applications. This guide covers indexing strategies (composite indexes, covering indexes), query optimization using EXPLAIN, avoiding N+1 queries, connection pooling, read replicas, and caching strategies. Apply these techniques to keep your application fast even as your data grows to millions of records.

blog.title: React State Management: From Context to Redux
blog.excerpt: A practical comparison of React state management approaches — Context API, useReducer, Zustand, and Redux Toolkit — with use cases.
blog.content: Choosing the right state management approach in React depends on your application's complexity. This article compares useState for local state, Context API for simple global state, useReducer for complex state logic, Zustand for lightweight global state, and Redux Toolkit for large-scale applications. Includes practical examples and migration strategies between approaches.

blog.title: Securing Your Laravel Application: Essential Practices
blog.excerpt: Protect your Laravel application against common vulnerabilities including SQL injection, XSS, CSRF, and mass assignment.
blog.content: Security should be a priority from day one. This article covers Laravel's built-in security features: CSRF protection, XSS prevention, SQL injection prevention via Eloquent, input validation, mass assignment protection, authentication best practices, password hashing, encryption, and security headers. Also covers rate limiting, logging, and monitoring for suspicious activity.

blog.title: Git Workflow for Teams: From Beginner to Advanced
blog.excerpt: Learn effective Git workflows including Git Flow, GitHub Flow, branching strategies, code review practices, and CI/CD integration.
blog.content: A solid Git workflow is essential for team productivity. This guide covers Git Flow for release management, GitHub Flow for continuous deployment, feature branching strategies, meaningful commit messages, pull request best practices, resolving merge conflicts, and integrating Git with CI/CD pipelines using GitHub Actions.

blog.title: Laravel Testing: From Unit Tests to Browser Tests
blog.excerpt: Master Laravel testing with PHPUnit, feature tests, HTTP tests, database tests, and Laravel Dusk for browser automation.
blog.content: Testing is crucial for maintaining code quality. This guide covers PHPUnit setup in Laravel, writing unit tests for classes and methods, feature tests for HTTP endpoints, database testing with factories and seeders, mocking external services, and browser testing with Laravel Dusk. Learn TDD practices and how to achieve high test coverage.

faq.question: What is your main area of expertise?
faq.answer: I specialize in backend development with PHP and Laravel. I build REST APIs, SaaS platforms, ERP systems, custom dashboards, and database architectures. I also work with frontend technologies like React and Tailwind CSS for full-stack projects.

faq.question: What technologies do you use?
faq.answer: My primary stack is PHP/Laravel for backend, MySQL for databases, and Bootstrap/Tailwind CSS for frontend. I also work with React, Next.js, jQuery, Docker, Git, and Linux servers.

faq.question: What kind of projects have you worked on?
faq.answer: I have built SaaS platforms (DivinDeep, Clinic SaaS), ERP systems, e-commerce platforms, law firm management systems (cabinet avocats), laundry service management (Afrilavage), task management tools, and more. My projects serve real clients in production.

faq.question: Do you have experience with REST APIs?
faq.answer: Yes, I have extensive experience designing and building RESTful APIs with Laravel. I implement JWT authentication, API versioning, rate limiting, request validation, and comprehensive documentation using OpenAPI/Swagger standards.

faq.question: What is your development workflow?
faq.answer: I follow a structured workflow: requirements analysis → database design → backend development → frontend integration → testing → deployment. I use Git for version control, Docker for environment consistency, and follow clean architecture and SOLID principles.

faq.question: Do you work with databases?
faq.answer: Yes, I design and optimize MySQL databases as a core part of my work. I create ERDs, write efficient queries, implement indexing strategies, and design normalized schemas with proper relationships for performance and data integrity.

faq.question: Can you build a complete website from scratch?
faq.answer: Yes, I can handle the entire development lifecycle from requirements gathering to deployment. I build both the backend (APIs, database, business logic) and frontend (responsive UI, state management) for complete web applications.

faq.question: How do you handle project communication?
faq.answer: I maintain clear and regular communication throughout the project. I provide progress updates, discuss requirements, show demos of completed features, and am available via WhatsApp, email, or video calls. I'm fluent in Arabic, French, and English.

faq.question: What are your rates?
faq.answer: My rates depend on the project scope, complexity, and timeline. I offer both fixed-price projects and hourly rates. Contact me with your project details and I will provide a tailored quote.

faq.question: Do you offer post-launch support?
faq.answer: Yes, I provide ongoing maintenance and support after project launch. This includes bug fixes, performance monitoring, security updates, and feature enhancements as your business grows.
